name: high-context
description: High-context vs low-context communication. Positive prompts rely on shared understanding, implicit meaning, and reading between the lines. Negative prompts spell everything out explicitly and literally.

pairs.positive: You know how these things go.
pairs.negative: Based on our previous three attempts, each of which failed due to budget overruns, I expect this one will also exceed the allocated funds.

pairs.positive: The situation at the office is... well, you've seen it yourself.
pairs.negative: Three people quit this month, morale is low, and the manager refuses to address it. That's the situation at the office.

pairs.positive: She gave me that look when I mentioned the deadline.
pairs.negative: She frowned, crossed her arms, and said she was unhappy about the deadline being moved up.

pairs.positive: I noticed you ordered the same thing as last time.
pairs.negative: I noticed you ordered the pasta again. Are you choosing it because you enjoyed it last time, or because nothing else on the menu appeals to you?

pairs.positive: Well, it's certainly not the worst idea I've ever heard.
pairs.negative: The idea has significant flaws. Specifically, the cost estimate is too low and the timeline ignores the regulatory review period.

pairs.positive: We both know why he didn't get invited back.
pairs.negative: He wasn't invited back because he argued with the host, spilled wine on the carpet, and left without helping clean up.

pairs.positive: The proposal is... interesting. Let's discuss it further next week.
pairs.negative: The proposal has three major problems that need to be fixed before I can approve it. I'll send you a detailed list today.

pairs.positive: I'm fine. Really.
pairs.negative: I'm upset because you forgot our anniversary, and I'd like to talk about it directly.

pairs.positive: Things have been complicated lately, as you can imagine.
pairs.negative: I'm going through a divorce, my car broke down, and I was passed over for promotion. That's what's been going on.

pairs.positive: He's the kind of person who always manages to find the last parking spot.
pairs.negative: He's selfish and inconsiderate. He takes advantage of situations without thinking about others.

pairs.positive: The neighbors have been... neighborly again.
pairs.negative: The neighbors played loud music until 2 AM last night and left their garbage bins blocking our driveway again.

pairs.positive: I trust you'll handle this the way it needs to be handled.
pairs.negative: Please fire the underperforming contractor, renegotiate the lease, and submit the revised budget by Friday.

pairs.positive: Remember what happened the last time we rushed something like this?
pairs.negative: The last time we rushed a product launch, we shipped with a critical bug that cost us two major clients and $300,000 in refunds.

pairs.positive: She didn't say no, but she didn't exactly say yes either.
pairs.negative: She said she needs more time to evaluate the offer and will give a definitive answer by next Wednesday.

pairs.positive: It's been one of those weeks, you know?
pairs.negative: This week I worked overtime every day, my child got sick on Tuesday, and my car needed an emergency repair on Thursday.

pairs.positive: The weather matches the mood around here lately.
pairs.negative: Everyone in the office has been stressed and irritable since the layoff announcement last Monday.

pairs.positive: You could hear a pin drop after he said that.
pairs.negative: Everyone was shocked and silent after he announced his resignation. No one responded for about thirty seconds.

pairs.positive: They did what they always do.
pairs.negative: They ignored the feedback, proceeded with the original plan, and blamed others when it didn't work out.

pairs.positive: We should probably talk about the elephant in the room.
pairs.negative: We need to discuss the fact that the project is three months behind schedule and 40% over budget.

pairs.positive: She's been spending a lot of time at the office lately, if you know what I mean.
pairs.negative: She's been working late because she's preparing for the product launch on March 15th and still has twelve deliverables outstanding.

pairs.positive: He thanked me in that way that means the opposite.
pairs.negative: He said thank you sarcastically because he was angry that I submitted the report without consulting him first.

pairs.positive: The apple doesn't fall far from the tree with that family.
pairs.negative: The son exhibits the same poor financial habits as his father: overspending, avoiding budgets, and taking on unnecessary debt.

pairs.positive: I'll leave it to your judgment. You understand the dynamics.
pairs.negative: Please decide which vendor to choose based on these three criteria: cost, delivery speed, and quality ratings.

pairs.positive: The dinner conversation took an unexpected turn, as these things sometimes do.
pairs.negative: During dinner, my uncle brought up politics, my sister disagreed loudly, and my father left the table.

pairs.positive: We all know what happens when corporate gets involved.
pairs.negative: When corporate gets involved, they add three approval layers, extend the timeline by six weeks, and reduce the budget by 20%.

pairs.positive: The smile she gave the waiter said everything.
pairs.negative: She smiled at the waiter flirtatiously because she found him attractive and wanted his attention.

pairs.positive: Let's just say the renovation didn't go as planned.
pairs.negative: The renovation went $15,000 over budget, took three extra months, and the contractor left the kitchen unfinished.

pairs.positive: He has a way with numbers, if you catch my drift.
pairs.negative: He manipulates financial reports to make performance look better than it actually is.

pairs.positive: Some things are better left unsaid at this point.
pairs.negative: I'm choosing not to discuss the CEO's affair because bringing it up now would derail the board meeting and accomplish nothing.

pairs.positive: There's a reason they serve the coffee so strong around here.
pairs.negative: The staff are overworked and exhausted, pulling twelve-hour shifts regularly, so they rely on caffeine to stay functional.
